implementation_path_note: The Agent tool for spawning a fresh base:integration-architect subagent is not exposed to the architect role in this harness. The team-lead playbook calls for delegation; the architect implemented directly given the small surface (3 source files + 1 test file) and the hard ordering constraint (subsequent stories depend on this wire-format change).
summary: Profile rumor wire format upgraded to carry a stringified, fully signed Nostr kind:0 event (SignedProfileEvent) inside rumor.content. serialiseProfileUpdate becomes async and takes an EventSigner; parseProfilePayload verifies the embedded sig synchronously via nostr-tools verifyEvent and falls back to the legacy flat shape; payloadToMemberProfile threads signedEvent through to MemberProfile and keys MemberProfile.pubkeyHex from the verified author rather than the caller-supplied fallback. MarmotContext gains a signerRef populated in init() and consumed by all 5 existing call sites. The PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND dispatcher arm enforces strict author-identity binding: signedEvent-bearing rumors are dropped if the inner pubkey disagrees with the outer MLS sender. Outer MLS rumor stays unsigned per MIP-03.
acceptance_criteria_satisfied: AC-001 — serialiseProfileUpdate(profile, signer) async, signs kind:0 draft, returns JSON-stringified SignedProfileEvent
AC-002 — parseProfilePayload verifies embedded sig via verifyEvent and returns ProfilePayload with signedEvent populated
AC-003 — parseProfilePayload returns null when sig fails verification; no caller-visible profile fields exposed
AC-004 — legacy flat ProfilePayload still parses with signedEvent === undefined
AC-005 — payloadToMemberProfile threads signedEvent through (conditional spread keeps it exact-optional)
AC-006 — SignedProfileEvent exported from app/src/types/index.ts with literal kind: 0
AC-007 — MemberProfile gains optional signedEvent?: SignedProfileEvent
AC-042 — all 5 existing serialiseProfileUpdate call sites in MarmotContext.tsx await the async result and pass signerRef.current; tsc --noEmit zero errors
AC-044 — app/tests/unit/marmot/profileSync.test.ts covers sign/verify round trip and legacy fallback (now 12 cases)

AC-047 — payloadToMemberProfile keys MemberProfile.pubkeyHex from signedEvent.pubkey when present (helper-level invariant); MarmotContext PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND dispatcher arm drops rumors where signedEvent.pubkey disagrees with the outer MLS sender (dispatcher-level guard) — added in remediation round 2 per Codex HIGH/MEDIUM findings

remediation_round_2.trigger: Verifier round 1 forwarded a Codex adversarial-review HIGH (dispatcher keyed by outer MLS sender, not by verified inner signedEvent.pubkey — latent for first-hand sends, broken for story 06 relay-on-behalf seam) plus a MEDIUM (payloadToMemberProfile signature lets callers pick the wrong identity).

files_changed.delta: payloadToMemberProfile parameter renamed to fallbackPubkeyHex; helper now keys MemberProfile.pubkeyHex from `payload.signedEvent?.pubkey ?? fallbackPubkeyHex` so a relayer or any future consumer cannot overwrite the author identity by passing a different fallback.

PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND dispatcher arm at lines 551-590 enforces strict author binding: when signedEvent is present and signedEvent.pubkey !== senderPubkey, the rumor is dropped (logged warn, no IDB write, no contact-cache write, no score-nickname write). On match, derives `authorPubkey = profilePayload.signedEvent?.pubkey ?? senderPubkey` and uses it for the contact-cache and score-nickname keys.

Added 2 cases: 'payloadToMemberProfile keys by signedEvent.pubkey, not the fallback (AC-047)' and 'payloadToMemberProfile keys by fallback when signedEvent is absent (legacy peers, AC-047)'. Total profileSync test count 12/12.
Added section N with new AC-047 covering both clauses (helper-level keying invariant + dispatcher-level mismatch drop).

regression_e2e.scope: targeted — 3 profile-related specs (re-run after round 2 changes)

remediation_round_2.policy_decision: Strict mismatch policy applied per verifier recommendation. For story 01, signedEvent-bearing rumors with outer/inner pubkey mismatch are dropped — there is no legitimate relay-on-behalf path in this story. Story 06 will introduce its own carve-out (likely a distinct rumor kind or an explicit relay flag) and re-evaluate the dispatcher gate then. Permissive (always-trust-inner-author) was rejected because for story 01 alone the two are equivalent and strict provides better defence-in-depth against a malicious peer wrapping arbitrary other-author profiles.

files_changed.delta: Added SignedProfileEvent type export; extended MemberProfile with optional signedEvent? field.

serialiseProfileUpdate async + EventSigner param; static-import verifyEvent from nostr-tools/pure; parseProfilePayload tries envelope first, falls back to legacy; payloadToMemberProfile threads signedEvent.

Added signerRef; populate in init() at line 268, null on cleanup; updated 5 call sites at lines 716 (proactive republish, slated for removal in story 04), 734 (onHistorySynced — wrapped in void async IIFE since callback signature is sync), 835 (createGroup), 944 (inviteByNpub), 1043 (publishProfileUpdate).

10 unit tests: PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND constant, round-trip, payloadToMemberProfile populated/undefined, legacy flat parse, forged-sig rejection, wrong-kind envelope, malformed inner content, malformed JSON, avatar round-trip.

full_e2e_groups.rationale: Per baseline.json.failure_acceptance, the verifier gate is 'zero NEW failures beyond pinned list'. Story 01 only changes the profile rumor wire format; it cannot affect any of the pinned-failure clusters (kind-443/30443 test bug, post-3dcc481 DM-reactions/reactions regressions, image-sharing, score-sync flake, seed-phrase flake). Targeted regression on the 3 profile specs covers the actual blast radius. Story 07 will run the full suite as part of the dedicated E2E story.

design_decisions_summary: Synchronous parseProfilePayload — verifyEvent imported statically from nostr-tools/pure (matches directMessages.ts pattern); avoids rippling async into the kind 0 receiver dispatcher arm at MarmotContext.tsx:546.

Signer access via signerRef = useRef<EventSigner | null>(null) populated in init() — single source of truth, cheap, gated by the existing client/pubkeyHex guard pattern. Boundary rule §3.5 preserved: no lib code instantiates a signer.

Backward-compatible parser — looksLikeEnvelope predicate distinguishes envelope (id/pubkey/sig/kind===0) from legacy flat (nickname/badgeIds/updatedAt) shapes; envelope branch verifies, legacy branch parses straight through with signedEvent undefined.

Type narrows kind:0 at the SignedProfileEvent type but assertion happens at the producer (serialiseProfileUpdate throws if signer returned non-zero kind) and at the consumer (looksLikeEnvelope rejects kind != 0).

onHistorySynced callback wrapped in `void (async () => { ... })()` rather than made async — preserves the sync `() => void` callback signature (matches existing pattern at MarmotContext.tsx:568-591 chat persistence).
out_of_scope_left_for_subsequent_stories: Story 02 — ProfileRequestPayload, kind 30, profileRequestSync.ts pure module, memo IDB store, mergeMemberProfile persistence of signedEvent
Story 03 — PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND dispatcher arm, target-side immediate reply, leaveGroup memo cleanup
Story 04 — remove proactive on-member-add republish at MarmotContext.tsx line 716 (this story leaves the line in place but updated to the new signature)
Story 05 — sweepStaleProfiles, app-start sweep effect, requestProfilesIfStale on context value, group-open sweep call from pages/groups.tsx
Story 06 — handleIncomingProfileRequest with backoff, notifyProfileObserved, pending-relay timer Map
Story 07 — full E2E spec for the six scenarios + helpers (rumor counter, IDB record-delete, page.clock retry harness)
verifier_review_notes: Compare post-implementation E2E failure set against baseline.json.e2e_groups.pre_existing_failures by spec:line. New entries are regressions; same entries are pinned; fewer entries is fine.
Confirm `cd app && npx tsc --noEmit` returns zero errors.
Confirm `make test-unit` reports 47 files / 644 tests / 0 failures.
Confirm targeted profile E2Es still green (the gate per architecture.json.tests.regression_e2e).
Spot-check that the outer rumor at MarmotContext line ~717/735/836/945/1044 still has empty tags and unsigned outer rumor (MIP-03 conformance — buildRumor unchanged).
Spot-check that the dispatcher at MarmotContext.tsx:546 still gates with `if (profilePayload)` and uses payloadToMemberProfile — both untouched.

ollama.verdict: skipped — local Ollama env not configured
ollama.note: OLLAMA_REVIEW_MODEL was not exported in the verifier session; team-lead authorized skipping Stage 1 and proceeding to Codex as the sole adversarial gate.

findings.title: Verified inner author is discarded; profiles are still keyed by the outer rumor sender
findings.text: parseProfilePayload now verifies the embedded SignedProfileEvent, but payloadToMemberProfile still accepts an external pubkeyHex and the PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND dispatcher was explicitly left unchanged. That means the cache key is still derived from the outer MLS rumor sender, not from signedEvent.pubkey. If any peer forwards another member's valid signed profile event, the receiver will merge that profile under the relayer's identity. This is exactly the identity split the new wire format was supposed to prevent, and it will break relay-on-behalf by poisoning member profiles/contact-cache with mismatched author data.

findings.title: Author-binding bug baked into the profile conversion helper
findings.text: payloadToMemberProfile takes a caller-supplied pubkeyHex instead of owning the author identity from the verified envelope. Correct handling depends on every caller remembering to switch from outer-sender identity to inner-author identity later — given the unchanged dispatcher, that has already gone wrong once.

rounds.acceptance: remediated in round 2 — see remediation_round_2 above; AC-047 added

findings.title: signedEvent.pubkey !== senderPubkey is bypassable because senderPubkey is still attacker-controlled JSON
findings.text: The new PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND branch only drops when the embedded signed author and outer rumor pubkey differ, then proceeds to merge/contact-cache/score-nickname writes on match. But the repo context for this epic explicitly says marmot-ts does not expose the authenticated MLS sender leaf, so senderPubkey here is just rumor.pubkey from the unsigned outer payload. That means the check is comparing one trusted value (signedEvent.pubkey) to another value the sender can forge. A malicious group member can wrap any valid SignedProfileEvent, set the outer rumor.pubkey to that same pubkey, and the rumor will pass this gate. Impact: the remediation does not actually close the wrapped-profile/impersonation seam it claims to block; receivers can still merge and cache profiles under an arbitrary author key as long as the attacker has a valid signed event for it. At minimum this still permits unauthorized relay/replay under another member's identity, and it can also pollute cross-group contact cache / nickname state for non-members if membership is not checked elsewhere. Recommendation: do not treat rumor.pubkey === signedEvent.pubkey as an authenticity check. Either block this path until authenticated MLS sender identity is available, or narrow the accepted surface: key exclusively by the verified inner signature, validate that the inner author is an actual member of the MLS group before any merge/cache write, and document that first-hand-vs-relay cannot be distinguished yet.

codex.acceptance: deferred — pre-existing MLS sender-pubkey trust gap explicitly out of scope per spec.md (lines ~30–35) and tracked separately in marmot-ts-rumor-sender-authentication.md. Story 01's mismatch guard prevents identity confusion in legitimate relays and acts as a policy gate so story 06 has a clean seam. The deeper MLS-membership check belongs in story 06 or in the marmot-ts upstream fix.
deferred_followups: Story 06 (or follow-up) — add an explicit MLS-group-membership check on signedEvent.pubkey before mergeMemberProfile / writeContactEntry / updateMemberScoreNickname.

Track via marmot-ts-rumor-sender-authentication.md — once marmot-ts surfaces the authenticated MLS sender leaf, replace the outer-pubkey comparison with a leaf-credential check across ALL rumor kinds (profile, chat, score, polls).

verifier_meta_finding: Process gap: the Agent tool with subagent_type 'base:verification-examiner' was not exposed to the verifier role in this harness (same gap the architect hit during implementation). Verifier substituted in-process Read+Grep examination for all VQs and confirmed Codex's findings directly against the source. Flagged to team-lead. Not a blocker for story 01.
